summary:
House Joint Resolution 90, introduced on April 3, 2025, by Representative Bice and referred to the House Committee on Agriculture, is a joint resolution proposing the congressional disapproval of a specific federal agency rule. 

The primary purpose of the resolution is to nullify the final guidance issued by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) titled "Commission Guidance Regarding the Listing of Voluntary Carbon Credit Derivative Contracts," which was published in the Federal Register on October 15, 2024 (89 Fed. Reg. 83378). 

Under the provisions of the resolution, which is brought pursuant to the congressional disapproval procedures in chapter 8 of title 5 of the United States Code, Congress would formally declare its disapproval of this regulatory guidance. The major legal effect of the resolution's passage is that the CFTC's guidance would be stripped of its authority, rendering it with no force or effect. The text of the resolution does not establish any exceptions, conditions, or specific deadlines.
